purpose: Compact Type A runtime doctrine frame: always-global active principles plus routed frequent/situational principles and candidate axiom pressure.
source_refs: obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed/raw_seed_principles.json
obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed/system_axiom_candidates.json

global_always.runtime_behavior: Safe to project as tiny/flag rows into every Type A runtime.

global_frequent.rule: status == active and scope_profile.domain == global, excluding global.constitutional_doctrine
global_frequent.runtime_behavior: Frequent cross-task pressure, routed by situation rather than always injected.

agent_principles.rule: status == active and scope_profile.scope_id in configured agent_principle_scope_ids
agent_principles.runtime_behavior: Agent-specific runtime projection: isomorphic principle rows, injected as ids/counts in entry strips and expanded only through the packet/card routes.

situational.rule: All other active principles; non-active rows stay out of runtime guidance.
situational.runtime_behavior: Open through --entry/--context-pack, paper modules, or explicit option-surface row drilldowns.

axiom_candidates.rule: Candidates remain candidate_not_active_doctrine and surface as provisional pressure only when task evidence matches.
axiom_candidates.runtime_behavior: Never inject as active law; use candidate_runtime_pressure and tape drilldowns.

global_runtime_capsule.authority_boundary: Active principles only; raw-seed principle graph remains authority.

candidate_axiom_pressure.non_law_warning: Candidate axioms are provisional pressure, not active doctrine; promotion is operator/controller governed.

rows.title: Actionability Drives Alerting
rows.tiny: alerts must back to actionable items
rows.flag: Status alerts and next-action hints must be derived from actionable item lists, never from raw lifecycle arithmetic.
rows.formal_clause: Any status surface that emits a next-action recommendation must be supported by at least one item in the corresponding actionable set.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

rows.title: Availability Before Invention
rows.tiny: route before authoring
rows.flag: Missing-affordance claims must walk the availability ladder before invention.
rows.formal_clause: Missing-affordance claims must route through the availability ladder before authoring or building.
rows.surface_when: An agent claims a missing affordance, proposes new doctrine / standard / skill / prompt / implementation, or asserts that something does not exist before checking the availability ladder.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

rows.title: Common Sense Up-Propagates
rows.tiny: local failures rise
rows.flag: Local failures must be checked for class-wide repair before being treated as one-offs.
rows.formal_clause: Local failures are samples of failure classes until bounded generalization proves otherwise.
rows.surface_when: A capable agent sees a local issue that smells general, or a primitive-choice task where external prior art may already encode adjacent solutions.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

rows.title: Context Is Discretionary Capital
rows.tiny: context is capital
rows.flag: Spend context intentionally by option surface, band, radius, and drilldown depth.
rows.formal_clause: Spend context by task-justified option surface, band, radius, and drilldown.
rows.surface_when: A command or agent packet is designed, reviewed, or used for navigation.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

rows.title: Cybernetic Projection Feedback
rows.tiny: project -> compare -> correct
rows.flag: Every action-bearing surface must project itself, read feedback, and route correction to its owning lane.
rows.formal_clause: Every durable surface that influences future action must emit a compressed self-projection, notice low-friction feedback signals, frame the implied mismatch against governing intent, evidence, and standards, and route correction or learning to the owning lane.
rows.surface_when: A task changes any action-bearing surface, adds an operator-facing projection, handles drift/audit/status output, or asks how agents and the operator share compressed system state.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

rows.title: Elegance Synthesizes Purpose
rows.tiny: unify repeated purpose
rows.flag: Repeated one-offs are evidence for a missing primitive.
rows.formal_clause: Repeated one-offs imply a missing unifying primitive.
rows.surface_when: A task proposes another surface, route, feature, or checklist around a repeated problem.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

rows.title: Evolution Proves In Microcosm
rows.tiny: prove in microcosm
rows.flag: Global grammar changes need bounded self-application proof.
rows.formal_clause: Global grammar changes require bounded self-application proof before migration.
rows.surface_when: A change proposes a new global grammar, standard, compiler, migration, or runtime lane.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

rows.title: High-Class Type B Is The Prefrontal Cortex; Type A Is The Rest Of The Brain
rows.tiny: B is prefrontal, A is brain
rows.flag: High-class Type B (operator-wielded reasoning model) is the prefrontal cortex; Type A is the rest of the brain. B characterizes shape; A explores and delivers. B response becomes A next prompt — B must remind A to look around.
rows.formal_clause: High-class Type B (operator-wielded extended-pro reasoning models, e.g., ChatGPT 5.5 Thinking via the Chrome bridge) is the prefrontal cortex of the cognitive system; Type A (Claude Code, Codex, deep-research agents in IDE/CLI) is the rest of the brain. Type B characterizes shape and routes; Type A explores deeply, delivers concretely, reports back. Because Type B response becomes Type A next prompt across an iterative manually-pasted loop, Type B must always encourage Type A to look around the system rather than narrowly literalize Type B inferred specifics, and must bias deliverables and shape-characterization over implementation over-specification. Four operational consequences for Type A return shape (operator voice 2026-05-23, evidence trail cap_quick_type_b_type_a_continuation_discipline_4_76128c6b98a0): (1) Specimen-first — classify the evidence shape of each incoming Type B paste (operating-protocol vs. trace vs. diff vs. workitem) before answering, so method-layer constraints are not mistaken for product-layer tasks; (2) Authority-boundary clarity — exact paths, ids, commands, standards, schemas are hypotheses unless evidenced by paste, hash, ledger row, or live disk read, and Type A distinguishes landed/surfaced/planned/claimed in its return; (3) Smallest deciding evidence ask — when private state would change the recommendation, request the smallest evidence slice that decides instead of broad audits or 'need repo access' caveats; (4) Ambitious reversible wave > tiny proof slice — take the largest coherent reversible safe wave inside the trust envelope and bind durable remainders to existing owners via Task Ledger quick-capture, instead of shrinking into permission-gated micro-slices.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

rows.title: Integration, Not Greenfield: Substrate Grows By Densification
rows.tiny: weave existing, don't greenfield
rows.flag: Half-built landscape is the substrate. Integrate before authoring; greenfield needs a coverage proof.
rows.formal_clause: Substrate grows by integrating existing partial surfaces; new parallel surfaces require explicit coverage-gap proof, not architectural sketches.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

rows.title: Meaning Is Relational
rows.tiny: meaning needs edges
rows.flag: Node meaning includes typed dependency neighborhood.
rows.formal_clause: No node is comprehensible without typed inbound and outbound edges.
rows.surface_when: A task depends on changing, compressing, routing, or trusting a node in isolation.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

rows.title: Operator Gesture Is The Seed
rows.tiny: gesture over literalism
rows.flag: Preserve evolving operator gesture, not fossilized sentences.
rows.formal_clause: Preserve evolving operator gesture over literal timelessness of any one utterance.
rows.surface_when: A task asks whether old raw-seed language should become doctrine or whether a projection may supersede voice.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

rows.title: Standards Are Shared Grammar
rows.tiny: standard as grammar
rows.flag: Artifacts should be written and read through the same standard.
rows.formal_clause: Write and read each artifact class through the same governing standard.
rows.surface_when: An agent is about to interpret, generate, or mutate a known artifact kind.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

rows.title: Classification Must Bind To Status
rows.tiny: classifications must reach status surfaces
rows.flag: A classification that affects future action must bind to the primary status surface or record a non-binding reason.
rows.formal_clause: A classification deposit that changes future actionability must bind to the primary status/audit surface that drives next-agent action, or record an explicit non-binding reason.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

rows.title: Total Substrate Descriptive Coverage
rows.tiny: every plane has an owner, route, and coverage state
rows.flag: Significant substrate planes should be descriptively covered; gaps become explicit debt.
rows.formal_clause: Every significant substrate plane should have at least one high-level descriptive owner, one routeable entry surface, and one explicit coverage state; missing coverage is named debt, not hidden context.
rows.surface_when: A task claims whole-system comprehension, route convergence, root navigation, frontend root rendering, or discovers a new substrate plane.
rows.non_law_warning: Candidate axiom; not active doctrine. Surfaceable in Type A operating packets as provisional pressure for failure-explanatory tasks.

routing_matrix.inject: tiny+flag into Type A runtime and bootstrap strips
ids/counts into Type A entry strips; full row only through agent operating packet or principle card
candidate label only when task evidence matches
